untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of head-and-shoulders patterns across 10 volatility-calibrated zigzag filter scales (ATR deciles: 5, 10, 15, 20, 30, 40, 50, 65, 80, 95%). The allowed indicator set lacks: (1) a zigzag filter implementation, (2) pattern detection capabilities for head-and-shoulders, and (3) multi-scale pattern confirmation logic. These are structural/algorithmic requirements beyond point-in-time indicator comparisons. ATR-based volatility normalization is available (atr_14), but the core pattern recognition and cross-filter logic cannot be expressed using only the permitted operators (>, <, >=, <=, ==, crosses_above, crosses_below, is) and single-indicator conditions.
translation_notes: This signal is architectural in nature—it prescribes a multi-scale pattern detection framework rather than discrete entry/exit trigger conditions. Implementation requires custom zigzag filtering, head-and-shoulders pattern recognition, and scale-dependent confirmation logic at the algorithm level, not at the rule-condition level.
